title: Darth Bandon
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Onslaught]]}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Bandonhead.jpg]] |name=Darth Bandon |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3956 BBY]],''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' in [[Hrakert Station]] on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{DB|character|darthbandon|Bandon, Darth}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=Bald |feathers= |eyes=Dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] {{C|formerly}} *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] {{C|formerly}} *[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] **[[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]] {{C|as Darth Bandon}} |masters=*[[Darth Bandon's Jedi Master|A Jedi]] {{C|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}} *[[Darth Malak]] {{C|[[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]]}} |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I have studied at the foot of the Dark Lord himself!|Darth Bandon, to Revan|}} '''Darth Bandon''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who defected to the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]], a conflict initiated by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]]. Sometime after Malak betrayed his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]], Revan, during an ambush by the Jedi and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] forces attempting to [[Capture of Darth Revan|capture]] them, Bandon became the new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] first [[Shadow Hand]], elevating him to second-in-command of the Sith Empire. When the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attacked]] the Republic warship ''[[Endar Spire]]'' above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], Bandon boarded the ship along with the [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] and [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] under his command and tried to capture the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bastila Shan]]. He [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Republic [[Ensign/Legends|ensign]] [[Trask Ulgo]], who sacrificed himself to save the former Sith Lord Revan, who had been mentally reprogrammed to be loyal to the Republic and to serve as a common [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. Shortly after the [[destruction of Taris]], the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Calo Nord]] was hired by Malak to follow in pursuit of Shan, who [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|escaped]] the bombardment with the help of Revan and his companions. After Nord was [[Ambush on Tatooine (Jedi Civil War)|slain]] by Revan, Malak dispatched Bandon to find and capture the Jedi. Bandon located Revan in Manaan's underwater [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]] harvesting base known as [[Hrakert Station]], on the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]]. The [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] confronted Revan and his allies after they found a fourth [[Star Map]] that would give them the location of the [[Star Forge]]—the ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] under Malak's control. Bandon underestimated the former Dark Lord and his companions, assuming that they would be no match for him. This proved to be his downfall, as Revan and his team [[Mission to Hrakert Station|vanquished]] Malak's Shadow Hand, along with the two Dark Jedi accompanying him. After his death, Bandon's head was severed from his body and preserved by the Sith. Three hundred years later, it had eventually been put on display in the [[Royal Museum of Alderaan|House Alde Royal Museum]] on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who would be known as [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Bandon was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], though he never fully accepted the teachings of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. Even though [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] flowed through him, Bandon's powerful emotions, such as anger and hatred, raged within and fueled his strength. His [[Darth Bandon's Jedi Master|Jedi Master]] tried to curb these strong emotions, but the brash Padawan chafed under his Master's guidance. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3959 BBY]], the former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]], the heroes of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], found the ancient [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as the [[Star Forge]]—a relic of the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] that had ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] nearly 20,000 years before—and named themselves the new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]]. Revan and Malak declared war on the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]{{InsiderCite|88|Heritage of the Sith}} and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], initiating the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Bandon rejected his old Master and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], fleeing to the revitalized [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|academy]] on the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. ===Jedi Civil War=== [[File:DarthMalakartwork.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Bandon's Sith Master, Darth Malak]] As a student in the new Korriban [[Sith academy|academy]], Bandon fully embraced the teachings of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and the [[Code of the Sith/Legends|Sith Code]]. This—along with Bandon's strength with the Force, his natural cruelty, and his utter ruthlessness in his quest for power—soon set Bandon apart from his fellow students and brought the attention of Darth Malak, Revan's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. Bandon eventually won Malak's admiration. {{CSWECite|I|57|Bandon, Darth}} [[3957 BBY|Two years]] into the Jedi Civil War, the Jedi and Republic forces set a [[Capture of Darth Revan|trap]] for Revan and Malak in an attempt to capture both Sith Lords.{{DB|character|darthmalak|Malak, Darth}}{{CSWECite|II|279|Malak, Darth}} During the battle, Malak betrayed his [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] by ordering his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']] to open fire on [[Darth Revan's flagship|Revan's vessel]]. After Malak thought he successfully [[Death/Legends|eliminated]] Revan, he seized his former Master's mantle of Dark Lord of the Sith. In the tradition of the [[Sith Order]], Malak sought a single learner among his followers to become his official apprentice, someone that could learn the Sith secrets and use them to effectively destroy the Jedi and the Republic.[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] Malak took the promising Bandon as his [[Shadow Hand]], which made him second only to his Master in the Sith Empire. The Dark Lord taught much of his knowledge of the Sith to Bandon. Malak was not disappointed with his choice of taking Bandon as his apprentice, because Bandon killed many Jedi during the war. In a short [[Time/Legends|time]], rumors began to surface that Bandon would challenge Malak for the right to rule the Sith; Bandon eventually began to set his sights on his Sith Master's throne. ====Raiding the ''Endar Spire''==== {{Quote|Damn–another Dark Jedi! I'll try to hold him off, you get to the escape pods! Go!|Trask Ulgo sacrifices himself against Bandon|}} [[File:DamnAnotherDarkJedi-KOTOR.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Darth Bandon prepares to face the Republic soldier Trask Ulgo.]] In the year [[3956 BBY]], Darth Malak was searching for a Jedi Knight named [[Bastila Shan]], fearing her strength in [[Battle meditation/Legends|Battle meditation]], a [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] that boosted the Republic forces' morale during battle while sapping the Sith's will to fight. When Malak learned of Shan's location aboard the Republic [[Warship/Legends|warship]] ''[[Endar Spire]]'', he sent a Sith battle [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to ambush the ship. Above the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], Bandon led the boarding party that [[Attack on the Endar Spire|stormed]] the ''Endar Spire'', in an attempt to capture or kill Shan. An amnesiac Revan—who had been rescued by Shan after Malak betrayed him and later mind-wiped by the Jedi Council—was placed under Shan's command as a common Republic [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]].''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''; [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] position in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] army is revealed in his second conversation with [[Carth Onasi]] regarding his background[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] Bandon confronted Revan and the Republic [[Ensign/Legends|ensign]] [[Trask Ulgo]] during their attempt to reach the ship's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], also hoping to ensure that Shan fled the ship alive. Bandon was given a chance to kill both Republic soldiers. However, before he could do so, Ulgo made a desperate charge, sacrificing himself so that Revan could get to the escape pods and flee the ship with the Republic [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Carth Onasi]]. After Bandon killed Ulgo, he departed the ''Endar Spire'' before [[Sith fighter|Sith interceptors]] destroyed the ship. Shan had successfully fled the ship aboard one of the escape pods and crash-landed on Taris. Revan and Onasi also crashed onto the [[Taris/Legends|Tarisian]] surface. In response, Malak [[Capture of Taris|captured]] Taris and ordered a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of the planet so that the Sith could search for Bastila Shan. ====Search for Bastila Shan and downfall==== {{Quote|My apprentice, Darth Bandon, shall take care of our young Jedi friend. ''[…]'' Find Bastila and bring her to me. Alive if possible.''"
"''As you command, Master.|Darth Malak and Darth Bandon|}} After Malak ordered the [[destruction of Taris]] in an attempt to kill Bastila Shan, Malak was informed by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Calo Nord]], who survived Taris' destruction, that Shan had successfully [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|escaped]] Taris; although, Nord was unaware that it was Revan, Malak's former Master, who assisted in her escape. Malak hired the bounty hunter to find and capture Shan, however, Nord was [[Ambush on Tatooine (Jedi Civil War)|killed]] by Revan during the Jedi's search for the Star Forge. The Sith [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] informed Malak of Nord's failure on Malak's flagship ''Leviathan'', and asked if he should hire another bounty hunter to send after Shan. The Dark Lord determined, however, that no mere bounty hunter could stand against a Jedi. Bandon was summoned to the ship's bridge immediately; when Bandon arrived on the bridge, he killed a [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith trooper]] that walked in his way. Malak sent Bandon after Revan, Shan, and their companions, and Malak ordered his apprentice to bring Shan to him alive, if possible. {{K1order}} [[File:BandonsLastStandOnManaan-KOTOR.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Bandon and his Dark Jedi confront Revan in Hrakert Station on Manaan.]] Bandon located Revan at the Republic's [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]] harvesting base known as [[Hrakert Station]], which was named for the [[Hrakert Rift]] in which the base was located, on the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] Manaan. The Shadow Hand confronted Revan and his allies in the station after Revan located a fourth Rakatan [[Star Map]], which was necessary to learn the location the Star Forge. Accompanied by two Dark Jedi, he presumed that Revan and his team would be no match for him. He expressed his fear that someone else had killed Revan, thereby depriving him the "pleasure" of slaying Revan. When Bandon was asked by Revan how he managed to make it to Hrakert Station, the Sith Lord replied he acquired an [[Submarine/Legends|underwater vessel]] and used the same route that Darth Revan and Darth Malak used underwater, leading him to the station. Bandon also told Revan that he was no match for the Sith apprentice, although Revan had defeated Calo Nord, whom Bandon thought was pathetic. Revan recognized Bandon, stating that he would pay for killing Trask Ulgo. The Sith Lord told Revan that his words meant nothing to him, unless he wished to beg for his life, and added that he would try to make Revan's death both quick and painful. Bandon and his Dark Jedi then [[Mission to Hrakert Station|engaged]] Revan and his companions in a duel. Despite Bandon's skill, he and the two Dark Jedi assisting him were killed by Revan and his allies. {{Endgame}} ===Legacy=== {{Quote|When Darth Bandon ultimately fell in battle, Sith acolytes preserved and enshrined his head. ''Despicable'' people.|Peema Ahuff|audio=WhyCantSithJustCollectStamps-TOR.flac|''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''}} After Bandon fell in battle and Revan's party left the scene, his remains were retrieved by a group of Sith acolytes who preserved his head through [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]]. Some time after Malak's death and the Sith Empire's collapse, the acolytes found themselves lacking leadership, and began to believe they could use the preserved head to communicate with Bandon's spirit. The acolytes eventually embarked on a misguided attempt to reattach the head to a new body, attracting the attention of the Jedi, who put an end to this cult. However, no trace of the head was found.{{TORcite|type=codex|The Head of Darth Bandon (Smuggler)}} [[File:PickledSith-TORCodex.png|thumb|right|Darth Bandon's preserved head]] Somehow the head ended up in the possession of [[Risha Drayen]] during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], and she used it during her quest to locate the treasure of her father, [[Nok Drayen]]. [[Peema Ahuff]], curator of the [[House Alde]] [[Royal Museum of Alderaan|Royal Museum]], wished to acquire the head and place it on display to honor [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] hero Trask Ulgo, who died in combat against the Sith Lord. Ahuff was willing to trade a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] engine for it, which Risha and her [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Voidhound|cohort]] needed, so the smuggler went to make the trade and learned that the gangster [[Skavak/Legends|Skavak]] had traded a fake head and absconded with the hyperdrive. The smuggler pursued Skavak's agents and retrieved the hyperdrive,{{TORcite|type=class|Smuggler|Ancient History|Alderaan}} then gave the real head to the museum, where it was put on display.{{TORcite|type=class|Smuggler|Payment Due|Alderaan}} At some point after Bandon's death, his [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]] also came into the possession of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|resurgent Sith Empire]], and was available for purchase by a well-trained Force-sensitive agent.{{TORcite|Item: "Darth Bandon's Double-Bladed Lightsaber"}} Bandon's role in the Jedi Civil War was remembered for millennia to come, though not always with perfect accuracy. According to an entry on the Jedi Civil War from the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Jedi [[Deesra Luur Jada]]'s [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], when Revan and Bastila Shan were [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|captured]] by the ''Leviathan'', Bandon was killed after Revan managed to escape. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the investigatory agent [[Gannod Chant]], who worked for the [[Council on Security and Intelligence]], translated a [[Wavlud Manuscript|manuscript]] written by suspected Sith sympathizer [[Ingo Wavlud]]. Wavlud's manuscript, however, stated that Bandon died in the year [[3951 BBY]] along with [[Darth Sion]] and [[Darth Nihilus]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You may have defeated the pathetic bounty hunter my Master sent after you, but you are no match for me!|Darth Bandon, to Revan|}} [[File:Bandonjuhani.jpg|thumb|left|Bandon dueling the Cathar Jedi Juhani]] During his time as a Sith Lord, the Human male known as Darth Bandon was known for his ruthless behavior. He was willing to take the lives of people and damage equipment to enforce his position, even for a perceived transgression as minor as walking in his way.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''; When [[Darth Malak]] tells Admiral [[Saul Karath]] that he would send Bandon after [[Bastila Shan]], Bandon walks on the bridge of the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], and kills a Sith soldier for walking in front of him He was also focused on the destruction of the Republic as well as the slaughter and extinction of the Jedi Order. Bandon thought that his skills were more than adequate to defeat Revan and his companions, telling the former Dark Lord that he was no match for him. Some in the Sith hierarchy felt that Bandon would eventually rise to challenge Malak for his mantle of Dark Lord, because of the Sith belief that the strongest must rule; and, it was also believed, that when the apprentice surpassed in ability the Master, the Master must fall. As time went by, Bandon grew arrogant, and he began plotting to overthrow Malak. Darth Bandon was light-skinned with dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The Sith Lord also had a bald head in addition to a brown goatee at the time of his death. ==Powers and abilities== Darth Bandon spoke and comprehended [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and [[Sith (language)|Sith]]. He was proficient in the [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|use]] of his double-bladed lightsaber, killing many of his former Jedi brethren. He also displayed his proficiency in using a single lightsaber when he dueled the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] Jedi [[Juhani]].''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''; [[:File:Bandonjuhani.jpg|Image of Darth Bandon dueling Juhani]] Before Bandon fought Revan, he had yet to meet his equal in combat. Because of his strength in the Force,{{InsiderCite|100|The 100 Greatest Things About Star Wars... Ever!|The 100 Greatest Things About ''Star Wars''... Ever!}} Bandon was capable of using many Force powers. He could use the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|push]] his opponents to the ground and could use it to alter the air currents around his opponent, turning it into a maelstrom. This swirling vortex of Force energy would lift an opponent into the air, spin them around, and completely immobilize them. Bandon was also able to use the Force to render himself virtually [[Force resistance|invulnerable]] to most offensive Force powers for a limited time, demonstrating this against Revan. Bandon's strength with the dark side of the Force enabled him to use dark-sided powers such as [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], and he also used the dark side to [[Force Enrage|tap]] into his rage and direct it against his opponents. He could use the Force to instill [[Mind trick/Legends|fear]] in his opponents, driving them temporarily insane. Bandon was capable of using the dark side to [[Force drain/Legends|drain]] his enemies' life-forces, and to strangle his opponents. He could also use it to [[Force Slow|debilitate]] his victims, having it work as a fast-acting toxin. After Bandon joined the Sith ranks, he constructed a saberstaff containing a red [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. His [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] resembled the one used by the Sith Lord [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], who initiated the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] [[3996 BBY|forty years]] before the end of the Jedi Civil War.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] Bandon also wore a customized set of [[Darth Bandon's fiber armor|light fiber armor]] which he fitted to his robes. ==Equipment== Darth Bandon wielded a double-bladed lightsaber with a red blade, and wore black torso armor over a set of dark blue [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:BandonConcept.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Concept art of Darth Bandon, which was later used as art of an armored Dark Jedi]] Darth Bandon was created by the developers at [[BioWare]] as a villain for the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', where he is voiced by [[Neil Kaplan]].{{LinkedIn|url=in/anyaisle|text=Neil Kaplan - Voice Over Artist - Abrams Artists Agency|archiveurl=https://archive.is/AeBdk}} Bandon's name was derived from the word "abandon."{{Blog|holocron|28|Darth Names and the Darth Who winner revealed.}} In the article ''"[[The 100 Greatest Things About Star Wars... Ever!]]"'', which was published in [[Star Wars Insider 100|''Star Wars Insider'' 100]], Bandon came in the twenty-second place, beating his Sith Master Darth Malak, who came in at twenty-third place. A picture of Darth Bandon in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' depicts him dueling the female Cathar Jedi Juhani. When [[Abel G. Peña]], who was one of the authors of the campaign guide, was asked on his official thread on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]] whether or not Juhani was present when Darth Bandon confronted Revan, Peña stated that it was "up in the air for the moment".{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=243200&start=80&tstart=0#17626838|thread=The Official Abel G. Peña Thread (Halagad Approved)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad]]|date=2009-05-09|time=2:02 PM}} A miniature figure of the [[Jedi Academy (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Jedi Academy'']] wave of ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'' resembles Darth Bandon, although the figure, released on [[June 30]], [[2009]], was simply named "Sith Apprentice."{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/jediacademyminis|text=''Jedi Academy''}} Bandon received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. Darth Bandon is referred to as a Sith Lord in the section detailing the Sith Order in the ''Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide''. However, both his entries in the campaign guide and the [[Databank (original)|Databank]] refer to him as a Sith apprentice. The guide [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] states that Revan killed Darth Bandon after escaping the ''Leviathan''. However, Bandon was defeated by Revan after he found the fourth Star Map, shortly before Revan and the crew of the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' were captured by the ''Leviathan''. The guide also states that Bandon died in the year 3951 BBY, along with Darth Traya, Darth Sion, and Darth Nihilus. However, Bandon was killed by Revan five years before in 3956 BBY. The planet on which Revan battles Bandon is determined by the order in which the Star Maps, which lead to the location of the Star Forge, are obtained by the player. Although canonically appearing on Manaan, Bandon appears on the planet where the fourth Star Map of the journey is found. If the player has already found three Star Maps, and for any reason returns to the site of one of the previous ones, Bandon will be confronted. Bandon is accompanied by two hooded Dark Jedi on Manaan or the desert world [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. If Bandon is encountered on the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] homeworld of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], he is accompanied by two Dark Jedi wearing armor. On Korriban, Bandon is accompanied by two students from the Sith Academy, the place where he was trained. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Darth Bandon}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{C|Head only}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Onslaught]]'' {{C|Head only}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{LucasArts|url=games/swkotor_sithlords|text=''Chronicles of the Old Republic''|int=Chronicles of the Old Republic}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'' *''[[Star Wars Annual 2008]]'' *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *{{InsiderCite|100|The 100 Greatest Things About Star Wars... Ever!|The 100 Greatest Things About ''Star Wars''... Ever!}} *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/FringeStatPack|text=Fringe Stat Pack}} {{Po}} *{{DB|character|darthbandon|Bandon, Darth}} *{{DB|character|darthmalak|Malak, Darth}} ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content={{Reflist}}}} ==External links== *{{SWTORWiki}} {{Interlang |de=Darth Bandon |es=Darth Bandon |fr=Dark Bandon |it=Darth Bandon |nl=Darth Bandon |pl=Darth Bandon |pt=Legends:Darth Bandon |tr=Darth Bandon }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Bandon, Darth}} [[Category:Darths]] [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Jedi trainees of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Sith Lords of Darth Revan's Sith Empire]]
